events.text: Patient is a 45-year-old Caucasian female with a family history of melanoma in her father, presenting with a rapidly growing pigmented lesion on her left calf.
At age 45, patient was diagnosed with nodular melanoma (ICD-O morphology 8721/3) of the skin of the lower limb (ICD-O topography C44.7), clinically staged as Stage IIIB (T4aN1aM0).

At age 45, excisional biopsy of the left calf lesion revealed nodular melanoma with Breslow thickness of 4.5 mm, Clark level V (invasion into subcutaneous fat), without ulceration, mitotic rate 8/mm2, no microsatellites, lymphovascular invasion present, margins involved.
At age 45, wide local excision with 2 cm margins was performed on the left calf with split-thickness skin graft closure.
At age 45, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the left inguinal region was performed, with 1 of 3 sentinel nodes positive for a micrometastatic melanoma deposit measuring 0.8 mm.
At age 45, sentinel lymph node biopsy pathology confirmed micrometastatic melanoma in 1 of 3 nodes, no extracapsular extension, confirmed by S100 and Melan-A staining.

At age 45, targeted next-generation sequencing panel of the primary melanoma identified BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 38%), NRAS wild-type, TP53 wild-type, TERT promoter mutation (C250T), and microsatellite stable status.
At age 45, staging PET/CT showed no evidence of distant metastatic disease and post-surgical changes in the left lower extremity and left groin.
At age 45, MRI brain with contrast was negative for intracranial metastases.
At age 45, baseline LDH was normal at 168 U/L, and after multidisciplinary tumor board discussion the patient was started on adjuvant pembrolizumab.
At age 45, adjuvant pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks was initiated for resected Stage IIIB melanoma per KEYNOTE-054 data.
At age 46, patient developed grade 1 hypothyroidism (TSH 12.4 mIU/L) likely immune-related, managed with levothyroxine supplementation without interruption of pembrolizumab.
At age 46, surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at 6 months was unremarkable with no evidence of disease recurrence.

At age 46, patient developed grade 2 immune-related hepatitis with ALT 185 U/L and AST 156 U/L, pembrolizumab was held, and oral prednisone 1 mg/kg was initiated with transaminase normalization over 4 weeks.
At age 46, pembrolizumab was resumed after hepatitis resolved to grade 0 and corticosteroids were tapered completely.
At age 46, surveillance PET/CT at 12 months showed no evidence of recurrent or metastatic disease with complete metabolic response.
At age 46, patient completed 12 months of adjuvant pembrolizumab, tolerating therapy well after hepatitis resolution, with LDH remaining normal at 155 U/L.
At age 47, surveillance CT at 18 months post-completion showed no evidence of disease recurrence and the patient remained in clinical remission.
At age 47, MRI brain surveillance was negative for intracranial metastases.
At age 47, patient continued routine surveillance every 3 months with physical examination and blood work showing LDH stable at 162 U/L with no evidence of recurrence at 24 months from diagnosis.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 45, excisional biopsy of the left calf lesion revealed nodular melanoma with Breslow thickness of 4.5 mm, Clark level V (invasion into subcutaneous fat), without ulceration, mitotic rate 8/mm2, no microsatellites, lymphovascular invasion present, margins involved.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 45, sentinel lymph node biopsy pathology confirmed micrometastatic melanoma in 1 of 3 nodes, no extracapsular extension, confirmed by S100 and Melan-A staining.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 45, targeted next-generation sequencing panel of the primary melanoma identified BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 38%), NRAS wild-type, TP53 wild-type, TERT promoter mutation (C250T), and microsatellite stable status.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 45, staging PET/CT showed no evidence of distant metastatic disease and post-surgical changes in the left lower extremity and left groin.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 45, MRI brain with contrast was negative for intracranial metastases.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 45, baseline LDH was normal at 168 U/L, and after multidisciplinary tumor board discussion the patient was started on adjuvant pembrolizumab.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 46, surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at 6 months was unremarkable with no evidence of disease recurrence.

Please correlate clinically.

Please correlate clinically.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

FINDINGS:
At age 47, surveillance CT at 18 months post-completion showed no evidence of disease recurrence and the patient remained in clinical remission.

At age 47, patient continued routine surveillance every 3 months with physical examination and blood work showing LDH stable at 162 U/L with no evidence of recurrence at 24 months from diagnosis.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Stage III/IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation. Treated with combination BRAF/MEK inhibitors (dabrafenib/trametinib) and/or checkpoint immunotherapy (nivolumab plus ipilimumab, pembrolizumab). Some with brain metastases. Include LDH levels.
